title: Schedule HVAC shutdown at 6 PM in Computer Science Block
description: Analysis shows Computer Science Block HVAC system continues running 2-3 hours after closing time. Implementing an automated shutdown schedule at 6:00 PM could significantly reduce energy waste without impacting occupant comfort.

title: Install motion-sensor lighting in Computer Science Block corridors
description: Corridor lights in Computer Science Block remain on 24/7. Installing PIR motion sensors could reduce lighting energy by 40% during low-occupancy hours, particularly overnight and weekends.

title: Implement waste segregation drive in Computer Science Block
description: Computer Science Block has a waste diversion rate below 35%. A targeted waste segregation awareness campaign and installing color-coded bins could improve recyclable recovery by 25%.

title: Upgrade to LED lighting in Computer Science Block
description: Approximately 60% of lighting fixtures in Computer Science Block use fluorescent tubes. Replacement with LED panels would reduce lighting energy consumption by 50% with improved lumen output.

title: Optimize Computer Science Block server room cooling
description: The server room in Computer Science Block is over-cooled, maintaining 18°C when 24°C is sufficient per ASHRAE guidelines. Raising the setpoint would reduce cooling energy by 15%.

title: Install solar panels on Computer Science Block rooftop
description: Computer Science Block has 15000 sqft of unshaded rooftop space ideal for a 50kW solar installation. This could offset 30% of the building's daytime energy consumption.

title: Reduce standby power in Computer Science Block labs
description: Lab equipment in Computer Science Block draws significant standby power overnight. Installing smart power strips with scheduled shutoffs could reduce phantom load by 60%.
